1. A laugh; laughter. 1. Ek chijarvā gāṅveṅ bikāë, Hindū, Musalmāṅ donoṅ khāë, Ādhā gadhā, ādhā khassī, Ye suni-ke mohe aile haṅssī . Bhoj. Riddle. Ans. Kharbūzā . Marshmelon. A thing sold in the village street, Hindu and Moslem both it eat, A donkey and a goat each half. When I heard this it made me laugh. 2. Khāṅsī bairī choran kī, hāṅsī baiyar kī . Prov. A thief's foe is a cough, a woman's foe a laugh. 3. Kyāhaṅsī ātī hai mujh ko hazrat-i-insān par, Fel-i-bad to khud kareṅ, lānat kareṅ shaitān par! Inshā. Lord of creation, man to laughter moves me just, Himself he evil does, the devil he curse must. 2. Jesting; flirtation. haṅsī uṛānā , v. a. To make fun or a jest of; to ridicule. haṅsī samajhnā, haṅsī meṅ le jānā , v. n. To take as a joke. Tum to haṅsī meṅ le jāte ho . Double entendre. haṅsī k. , v. a. 1. To laugh and jest. 2. To flirt; jest with. haṅsī khushī , adv. Gladly. haṅsī meṅ , adv. In jest. haṅsī meṅ uṛānā , v. a. To turn off with a joke. haṅsī meṅ khaṅsī h. , v. n. lit . to go from laughing to coughing. To go from words to blows.
s.f. (rustic or dialec.), Laughing, laughter, &c. (i.q. haṅsī, q.v.).

v. n. S. हसन; Pr. hassaī he laughs. 1. ( haṅsī ānā ) To laugh. Waqt kā ronā be-vaqt ke haṅsne se achchhā . Prov. Better weep in season than laugh out of season. 2. To laugh at; deride. 1. Jo haṅsegā voh haṅsāyā jāëgā . Prov. Who laughs will be laughed at. 2. Goiṭhā jale, gobar haṅse . Prov. Dried cow pats burn, the fresh dung laughs. 3. Sir turrā muqqaish kā, aur gorā kāman gāt, Khaṛī khaṛī rove haṅse, kanth na pūchhe bāt . Riddle. Ans. Shamā . A candle. A golden crest upon her head, a body fair she's got, She ever stands and weeps and laughs, her husband heeds her not. 3. To laugh and be merry. Ro-ke pūchh le, haṅs-ke uṛā de . Prov. With tears shed learnt your mind, alaughing him you find. 4. To jest or flirt with. 1. Haṅste hī ghar baste haiṅ . Prov. 'Tis laughter fills a house. (From flirting comes marrying.). (Flirt in jest, marry in earnest) 2. Haṅsiye dūr, paṛausī ke nāh . Flirt with strangers, not with your neighbours. 5. To be in jest, not earnest. Maiṅ to haṅstā thā . haṅsoṛ', haṅsokaṛ . One who is always laughing. Pat nāhīṅ nār haṅsokaṛ kī . Rus. song. A woman. who is always laughing is held in no respect.
v.n. (rustic or dialec.) = هنسنا haṅsnā, q.v.

n. m. 1. A name of Mahādeva. 1. Pattharpūje Harmileṅ to maiṅ pūjūṅ pahāṛ, Us patthar se chakkī bhalī jo pīs khilāë sansār . Kabīr. If stones can show us God I'll worship all the hill, The best of stones are mill-stones which men's stomachs fill. 2. Lābh binā nahīṅ Har ke . Prov. No profit's e'er without Har got. 3. Har jaise ko taisā . Prov. God gives to each as he does. 2. Water. 3. A frog. 4. A snake. Har bolā, har ne sunā, har gaë har ke pās, Woh har to har meṅ gaë, woh har bhaë nirās . The croaking frog the snake doth hear, and t'wards the frog he goes, The frog into the water jumps, despair the snake now shows. har gangā k . To take Har's name while bathing. har-bhajan , n. m. The worship of Har.
more: n. m. A plough. harā'ī , n. f. The furrows made by the plough.
n. f. The sound made in flying (a bird). hur ho jānā , v. n. 1. To fly away; take wing. 2. To disappear; vanish.

adj. pron. Each; every. 1. Har jaise ko taisā . Prov. Each gets as he does. 2. Har kāre o har mardė . Prov. A man for every work. har ādmī, har bashar, har shakhs, har koī, har kadām, har kas , Each; every person; every one. har-āṅ-kī, har-gāh ki . Whereas; seeing that. har-ek kī haisīyat , Respective means. har-bābī . A jack of all trades; a master of every art. har bār ; Rus. har ber . Every time; always. har-jāī , n. m. 1. A stroller; vagabond. 2. A flirt; an inconstant lover. 1. Āj mujh se hai to kal aur se, mirzā, ikhlās, Aise har-jāī se ho nauj, nigoṛā, ikhlās? Wom. Jān Sāhab. Today with me, tomorrow, Sir, you'll with another go, Never with such a worthless wretch will I have aught to do. 2. Tū to harjāī hai, apnā bhī yehī taur sahī, Tū nahīṅ, aur sahī; aur nahīṅ, aur sahī . Amānat. You wander, wanton, every where, I too will do as well, If not you then a new one, or a third will s…

n. f. S. हरीतकी 1. A myrabolan. 1. Haṛ, baheṛā, āṅvlā, chauthī ḍāl gilo, Pancham jīrā dāl-ke nirmal kāyā hoë . 2. Dūdh biyārī jo kare, niti uṭh harreṅ khāë, Moṭī datvan jo kare, tā ghar baid na jāë . Who milk for supper has, on waking eats myrabolan, A toothbrush stout employs, no doctor will his threshold span. 2. An ornamental tassel resembling the fruit. 3. The stocks.
n. m. Cont. of ﮩـارَ A bone. haṛ-phūṭan , n. f. Pain in the bones. haṛ-vāṛ , n. m. A family vault.

ety: Pārsī har; old P. haruva; Zend haurva; S. सर्व
def: adj. Every; each; any; — all
more: adj. Every, any. هرآن har ān, 1. Constantly. 2. Whenever. هرايک har ek, Every one, each. هرباب har bāb, Every chapter, all sciences, every circumstance. هربابي har-bāb-ī, Master of knowledge of every kind; a factotum. هربار har bār, Every time. هرجاٴي har-jā-ī, adj. Vagabond, stroller. هرحال har ḥāl, Howsoever. هردل عزيز har-dil-aﻌzīz, Beloved by every one, popular. هردم har dam, Every moment. هردم خيالي har-dam-ḵẖiyāl-ī, Whimsical, capricious. هرديگي چمچه har-degī-chamcha, A sponger, one who hangs on others for a maintenance, a busy meddling person. هرکاري har-kār-ī, Attending to all sorts of business. هرکدام har kudām, or هرکس har kas, or هرکوئي har koʼī, Every one, whoever. هرکہين har-kahīṅ, Every where. هرکيون har-kyūṅ, By every means, in every way. هرگاه har-gāh, adv. 1. Whenever, always. 2. Whereever, every where. هروقت har waḳt, At all times, always. هريک har yak, Every one, each,…
s. m. A rogue, a wag.

(-kā), 'The bones to break'; — to make progress or advancement, to advance
adj. & s.m. Scraggy; — a scraggy person, a bag of bones; a miserable object or creature
or har-wār, s.m. A place where bones are laid; a place of dry bones; a burial ground; a family vault; charnel-house.

def: s.m. The stocks, bilboes.
s.f. A myrobalan (i.q. harrā, or haṛṛā); — a tassel, or other ornament, resembling the myrobalan.
n. m. ( Gram .). A disjunctive conjunction.
s.m. A plough
subs: s.f. Worship of the plough (a ceremony performed on the day on which ploughing closes, when the plough is washed and decorated with garlands; — to use or to lend the plough after this day is considered unlucky)
s.m. Mutual assistance in ploughing; — a sharer in a plough

or har-sotīyā, s.f. The first furrow ploughed; the first ploughing of the season (it is generally preceded by the taking of omens, and other superstitious ceremonies); — affording assistance in ploughing; — the bringing home the plough
s.f. 'Land over which the plough has been drawn,' the cultivated lands of a village. (For other compounds see hal.)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هر (har). Doublet of سب (sab).

def: ( In Comp .) A doer, maker, Eng.-er, -ing, -man; as māran-hār a striker; lakaṛ-hārā a wood-man; jān-hār a dying-man.
more: n. m. S. हार 1. A necklace. 2. A wreath; chaplet; garland. 3. A string of jewels; carcanet. hār pahin'nā , v. n. To wear the laurel. hār ḍālnā , v. a. 1. To throw a garland round the neck. 2. To give one's hand in marriage. hār gūṅdhnā , v. a. To make a garland of flowers. gale kā hār h. , v. n. See under galā . To hang about one's neck.
n. f. 1. Fatigue; weariness. 2. Defeat discomfiture. 3. Forfeiture; loss. Divālī ke din kī hār baras din tak rakhtī hai . Prov. Who loses in Divālī will lose all the year. 4. Pasturage.
n. m. 1. Abone. 2. A skeleton. Hāroṅ thake, behvāroṅ thake . Prov. The bones ache, traffic fails.
n. m. 1. A crowd (انبوه). 2. Noise; uproar.

ety: Ap. Prk. अडं and अडअं (with euphonic h inserted); S. क+रं or र+कं
def: suff. (f. -ī), added to infinitives to form, 1˚ part. fut. act., or 2˚ nouns of agency (the inf. aff., in Western Hindī, generally takes the form an or ne = Prk.अणअ or अणिअ = S. अनीय; in East. H. ani or ni = Prk. अणिअ, &c.), e.g. karan-hār, or karne-hār or hārā (E. H. karani-hār or hārā), 'about to do; one who is going to do; doer'; — hon-hār, or honi-hār, 'what is to be; possible; future.' In W. H. this suff. frequently takes the form wālā or wārā (euphonic w taking the place of h), e.g. dene-wālā or dene-wārā (f. -wālī, &c.), 'about to give; one who is about to give; a giver.' The suff. hār and hārā are frequently confounded with the secondary suff. wālā, q.v.; e.g. lakaṛ-hārā, 'a woodman.' (It would perhaps be more correct to regard these suff. as being ˚anhār, ˚anehār, ˚anihār, &c., and to consider them as attached to the roots of verbs; e.g. mar-anhār, fr. rt. mar; but the popular notion is that the suffixes are hār, &c., and are joined to infinitives; and hence, probably, the confusion between these suffixes and the suffix wālā.)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit हारि (hāri), from Sanskrit हारि (hāri, “defeat; losing a game”).

def: s.f. (dialec.) = harrā, or haṛ qq.v. s.m. Attack, assault, onset, storm
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هر (har). Doublet of سب (sab).

def: s.m. Taking, seizing, &c.; — a porter, carrier; — war, battle; — a necklace, a string (of pearls, &c.); a garland or chaplet (of flowers); a wreath; — a flock (of cattle); — (in Arith.) divisor; denominator (of a fraction)
more: (in compos.) means the performer of any act, and corresponds frequently to the English terminations er or ing; as, بہارآٴي نه ديکهن هارآيا انارآيا نه چاکهن هارآيا Bahār āʼī na dekhan-hār āyā; anār āyā, na chākhan-hār āyā. The spring is arrived, but no one is come to behold it; the pomegranates are ready, but there is no one to taste them.

1. s. m. (s. हार ) A necklace of pearls, &c. a wreath, a chaplet (of flowers, worn as a necklace). 2. (for हारि ) A flock (of cattle). 3. Pasturage. 4. s. f. Loss, forfeiture, discomfiture. 5. part. act. (from هارنا) Being overcome. هارماننا يا مان لينا hār-mānnā, or -mān lenā, To give up a dispute, to give up in despair, to acknowledge all lost. هارجيت hār jīt, Gambling, hazard. هار جيت کرنا hār jīt karnā, To gamble.
subs: To put on a garland, or chaplet, or wreath, &c.
(kisī-ke gale-meṅ), To throw a garland (round the neck of anyone); — to give (a maiden) in marriage
(-ke), To be or become as a necklace (to), to hang round the neck (of a person).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit हारि (hāri), from Sanskrit हारि (hāri, “defeat; losing a game”).

def: n. m. A mud oven on which milk is boiled.

adj. S. हरित. 1. Green; verdant; fresh. 1. Gadhe ko harā hī harā sūjhe . Prov. In the donkey's eyes all seems green. 2. Kaho jo Rām kā kavan sā rūp hai, Yuvā dekhā yā birdh, bālā? Kaho jo Rām kavan isthūl hai, Harā, pīlā, ki lāl, kālā? Kabīr. Tell me, can you what is Ram's body like, Or young, or old, or infantine belike? What aspect, what complexion doth he wear? Or green, or yellow, red, or black, or fair? 2. Blooming; flourishing. 3. Green (a wound). 4. Half raw or unbaked. har'ā , n. m. 1. Green color. 2. Green food for cattle. harā bāg , n. m. 1. A green garden. 2. ( Met .) Delusive hope. 3. ( Bot .) Herbaceous. harā bharā, harī bharī , adj. 1. Luxuriant; fruitful. 2. Prosperous; happy. Terī god sadā harī bharī rahe! Benediction. harā h., haryānā , v. n. 1. To be green. 2. To be promising. Ḍūbe rupaë hare ho gaë . 3. To be refreshed, exhilarated. Nahāne se jī harā ho jātā hai . 4. To be overjoyed, pleased, glad. Māṅ beṭe ko dekh-kar harī ho gaī . 5. To break out afresh. hare bhare raho! May you live and prosper! Heaven bless you! harī tarkārī , n. f. Vegetables; green meat. harī fasl . Green crops, as cucumbers, potatoes, carrots, and other garden produce. hare meṅ ānkheṅ h., yā phūlnā , v. n. To be prodigal, extravagant.
more: n. m. Dispersion (of an army, assembly, etc.); general jail delivery.

def: s.m. A necklace, a string of pearls, &c. (i.q. hār, q.v.). s.m. Taking, seizing, &c.; — a porter, carrier; — war, battle; — a necklace, a string (of pearls, &c.); a garland or chaplet (of flowers); a wreath; — a flock (of cattle); — (in Arith.) divisor; denominator (of a fraction)
subs: To put on a garland, or chaplet, or wreath, &c.
(kisī-ke gale-meṅ), To throw a garland (round the neck of anyone); — to give (a maiden) in marriage
(-ke), To be or become as a necklace (to), to hang round the neck (of a person).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A mud oven (on which, milk, &c. are boiled).

ety: Prk. हरियओ or हरिअओ; S. हरित+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Green, verdant; fresh; — gladsome; — blooming, flourishing; — green (as a wound); — half-raw; unbaked; — s.m. Green colour; — green food (for cattle)
subs: s.m. A green garden; — a blooming garden; — (met.) delusive hopes; — adj. (in Bot.) Herbaceous
v.t. To show one a blooming garden; — to raise delusive hopes in; to delude
adj. (f. harī-bharī), Luxuriant; fruitful; — overgrown; — prosperous, happy
May you prosper and be happy

v.n. To be or become green; — to bloom; — to be promising (e.g. ḍūbe rupaʼe hare ho-gaʼe); — to be refreshed, be exhilarated; — to become delighted, or pleased, or glad; — to become raw (as a wound); to break out afresh
s.f. Green crops, garden-produce (as cucumbers, potatoes, carrots, and the like)
'To have (large) eyes (or the eyes to expand) in prosperity'; to become extravagant or prodigal.
wety: From Sanskrit हरित (harita).

def: s.m. A sky-rocket.

ety: Ap. Prk. अडं and अडअं (with euphonic h inserted); S. क+रं or र+कं
def: suff. (f. -ī), added to infinitives to form, 1˚ part. fut. act., or 2˚ nouns of agency (the inf. aff., in Western Hindī, generally takes the form an or ne = Prk.अणअ or अणिअ = S. अनीय; in East. H. ani or ni = Prk. अणिअ, &c.), e.g. karan-hār, or karne-hār or hārā (E. H. karani-hār or hārā), 'about to do; one who is going to do; doer'; — hon-hār, or honi-hār, 'what is to be; possible; future.' In W. H. this suff. frequently takes the form wālā or wārā (euphonic w taking the place of h), e.g. dene-wālā or dene-wārā (f. -wālī, &c.), 'about to give; one who is about to give; a giver.' The suff. hār and hārā are frequently confounded with the secondary suff. wālā, q.v.; e.g. lakaṛ-hārā, 'a woodman.' (It would perhaps be more correct to regard these suff. as being ˚anhār, ˚anehār, ˚anihār, &c., and to consider them as attached to the roots of verbs; e.g. mar-anhār, fr. rt. mar; but the popular notion is that the suffixes are hār, &c., and are joined to infinitives; and hence, probably, the confusion between these suffixes and the suffix wālā.)
more: (in compos.) denotes the performer of any act, dealer in any article. It is often represented in English by the word man, or the termination er; as لکڙهارا lakaṛ-hārā, A woodman. مارني هارا mārne-hārā, A striker.
s. m. A necklace of pearls, &c. See هار hār, signif. 1.

def: perf. part. of harnā, 'to seize,' &c.
wety: From Sanskrit हरित (harita).

def: adv. In the position of one beaten; under compulsion, &c. ( = hār-kar, q.v.).
s.m. (dialec.) 1˚ corr. of haṛ-wār, q.v.s.v. haṛ, 'bone'; — 2˚ corr. of harā-wal, q.v.
s.f. Turmeric, Curcuma longa.
more: s. f. Turmerick. (Curcuma longa). ¶ hrad ह्रद s. m. A deep lake, a large or deep piece of water.
s. m. Heart, breast, mind, soul, life.

def: s.f. = haṛhaṛī, or huṛhuṛī, q.v. s.f. The twang of a bow; — hurry, hurry-skurry; — crowd, throng, crush.
more: s. f. The twang of a bow.

def: s.m. Spirituous liquor, wine.
s.f. A grape.
s.f. Losing (at play, &c.); loss.
s.f. Defeat, overthrow; foiling, worsting; harassing, worrying; tiring out, wearying; teasing, boring, bothering
subs: or haraunī-jitaunī karnā, v.t. To harass, worry, annoy, tease, &c.

ety: harā, q.v.+Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Greenness, verdure; — vegetation; — freshness; — gladsomeness, &c. (see harā).
s.f. Furrow made by a plough; ploughing (i.q. halāʼī): — the portion of land in a field which is included within one circuit of a plough.
s. f. Greenness, freshness, verdure.
Perforce; willy nilly. Apnī harāī ko āp hī le jāëgā. Nolens volens he'll have it.
s.m. = P حرجت ḥarajat, s.f. (for A. حرجة ḥarajat, inf. n. of un. of حرج 'to be collected'), A collection of trees, a wood, a thicket; — a collection or flock of camels.
s.f. = P حرجه ḥaraja, s.m. (for A. حرجة ḥarajat, inf. n. of un. of حرج 'to be collected'), A collection of trees, a wood, a thicket; — a collection or flock of camels.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Taking, seizing, carrying off; — one who seizes or carries off, a plunderer, thief; a cheat, rogue; a gambler; — the tree Trophis aspera; — (in Arith.) a divisor.
s.m. Taker, conveyer, seizer; — an epithet of Śiva; — a thief; rogue, cheat; — (in Arith.) division; divisor: — a reflecting or judicious person.
s.f. Hankering (after), craving; fretting, &c. (i.q. haṛkā); — hydrophobia
subs: v.n. To have an attack (or a fit) of hydrophobia; — haṛak lagnā (-kī), To have (or feel) a longing (for), to crave.

def: s.f. The three short vowels
subs: s.f. 'Alternate movements and rests'; gestures, gesticulation, postures.

ety: for A. حرکة ḥarakat, fem.; v.n. fr. حرك 'to move'
def: s.f. Motion, action, movement, proceeding; gesture, gesticulation; act, deed; an improper or bad action, misdemeanour, fault, minor offence, crime; fornication, adultery; — agitation, commotion; opposition, interruption, prevention, hindrance; loss, damage; — a short vowel, a vowel-point
more: n. f. Pl. harakāt . 1. Motion; movement; gesture; gesticulation. 2. An act; deed. 3. Illicit intercourse. 4. Agitation, especially of an objectionable kind, as opposition, hindrance, interruption. 5. Misdemeanor. 6. Fault; slight offence; crime. 7. Loss; damage. 8. (A. erāb ) A short vowel; a vowel point (H. mātrā ). 9. Indisposition. Illit. harakāt-i-salāsah , n. f. The three short vowels. harakāt o sakanāt , Ped. lit . Alternate movements and rests. Gesture; gesticulation. harkat-i-bejā . ( Law ) A wrongful act. harkat denā ; Ped. mutaharrik k. , v. a. To move; set in motion. harkat-i-rozānah sitārah ; S. दैन गति. Daily motion of the planets. harkat-i-sālānah ; S. बार्षिक गति Annual motion. harkat kā bāis h. , v. n. To cause or produce motion. harkat k. , v. a. 1. To move; act; work; do. 2. To hinder; impede; disturb; interrupt. 3. To do any bad act; misbehave. 4. To have illicit sexua…
s. f. 1. Motion, procedure, gesture, action (usually improper). 2. Prevention, hinderance, interruption. 3. A short vowel. حرکت کرنا ḥarakat karnā, To move, to do or to commit an improper action.
subs: (-ko), To set in motion, to move

v.n. To move, act, work; to move (in, -meṅ); to do any bad act, act improperly (in respect of, -meṅ); to commit fornication or adultery; — to practise interruption, to interrupt, hinder, obstruct, disturb
v.n. To be interrupted, be disturbed, be hindered, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Interrupting, obstructive; — an interrupter, &c.

n. m. S. हर्ष Pleasure; delight. Harakh uṭhe ādar kare āvat jān atīt, Tulsī! tab hī jāniye Parmeshvar se pīt . Tulsī Dās. Who rises pleased and shows respect to friars when they come, Tulsī! in such men know the love of God doth ever bloom.
s.m. = harsh, q.v. s.m. Thrill, rapture, delight, glee, joy, pleasure, gladness, elation, exultation; — expanding, blowing, blooming; — adj. (f. -ā), Joyful, delighted, elated, glad, happy
more: s. m. 1. Blowing, blooming. 2. Delight, joy, happiness, pleasure.
subs: adj. & s.m. Causing delight, gratifying, delighting, gladdening, exhilarating; delightsome; — rejoicer, gladdener, delighter, &c.
v.n. To be delighted, to rejoice, &c.
harsh-mant, harsh-vān, harsh-vat, or harsh-vant, adj. Joyful, delighted, elated, happy
(˚sha+ud˚), s.m. Rise of joy or bliss, occurrence of pleasure, dawn of happiness.

def: v. n. S. हर्षण To be pleased, delighted. Barkhat harkhat jagat sabh, pharkhat lakhe na koë , Tulsi Das. Tulsī! tāhī sarāhië bhūp bhānu sam hoë . God's rain on all the earth doth fall, no difference you see, Tulsi! blessed be the king who shines on all as Sun's rays free!
v.n. = harasnā = harshnā, qq.v. v.n. (dialec.) = harshnā, q.v. v.n. To be delighted, or glad, or pleased; to rejoice, exult; to be happy; — to blow, to bloom (as a flower).
more: v. n. 1. To blow (as, a flower). 2. To be delighted.

def: v. n. See ﮩرکانا
more: v. n. See ترسنا To fret; pine.

def: v.n. (dialec.) = هڙکنا haṛaknā, q.v.
more: v. n. 1. (from Arab. حرک) To stop. 2. (from Arab. حرق) To be scorched.

def: v.n. To feel a great desire (for), to have a longing (for), to long, crave; — to grieve (for), to fret, pine; — to be driven away; to be kept at a distance; to be estranged; — to be discouraged, or deterred; — to stop; — to remove; — to be scorched (in this sense haraknā is the usual form).
s.m. = hāriyal, q.v. adj. Of a green colour, green; — s.m. The green pigeon, Columba hurriala (i.q. hariyāl, or hariyal, q.v.).

ety: inf. n. of حرم 'to be prohibited,' &c.
def: adj. Forbidden, prohibited, unlawful, wrong; excluded; sacred, inviolable; — illegitimate, spurious ( = ḥarām-zāda); — disingenuous, dishonest; — s.m. An unlawful act, wrong-doing, iniquity; fornication, adultery; — a wrong-doer; fornicator, &c. ( = ḥarām-kār and ḥarām-kārī)
more: adj. حرم was unlawful. 1. Illegitimate; adulterous. 2. Unlawful; forbidden; unclean; impure. 1. Khudā kā mārā harām, apnā mārā halāl . Prov. Struck dead by Heaven unlawful meat, slaughtered by man it is food meet. 2. Do mullāoṅ meṅ murgī harām . Prov. Between two reverend doctors the fowl becomes unlawful meat (through being torn to pieces in the struggle for possession). 3. Harām khānā, aur shalgam! Hin. Prov. Eat forbidden food, and only a turnip! (One may as well be hanged for a sheep as for a lamb). 3. Wicked; wrong; nefarious. harām haddīs , Lawful and unlawful. harām-khor, harām-khānā , n. m. Opp. of halāl-khor . 1. A venal or corrupt person; one who lives on the wages of iniquity; one addicted to taking bribes, or other unlawful gains. 2. An idle, slow, or lazy person who does not perform the task for which he is paid. harām-khorī , n. f. 1. Peculation; embezzlement; malversation;…

n. m. Adultery (Sant. lāṭ ); illicit intercourse. 1. Harām koṭhe chaṛh-kar pukārtā hai . Prov. The harlot cries from the housetop. 2. Harām chālīs ghar le-kar ḍūbtā hai . Prov. The adulterer sinks with forty ruined households on his back. 3. Harām meṅ baṛā mazā hai . Prov. Sin is sweet.

(from حرم Heb. ) adj. Unlawful, forbidden, prohibited, excluded, sacred, wrong. حرام رکهنا ḥarām rakhnā, To prohibit. حرام خور ḥarām-ḵẖor or حرام خوار ḥarām-khẉār, s. m. One who lives on the wages of iniquity. حرام زاده ḥarām-zāda, adj. 1. Illegitimate, base-born, bastard. 2. s. m. A wicked rogue, rascal. حرام زادگي کرنا ḥarām-zāda-gī karnā, To abuse, to ill treat. حرام مغز ḥarām-mag̠ẖz, Spinal marrow. حرام کار ḥarām-kār, s. m. A fornicator. حرام کاري ḥarām-kār-ī, s. f. Forbidden acts (particularly, adultery and fornication). حرام کهانا ḥarām khānā, To receive the wages of iniquity.
subs: ḥarām-ḵẖor, s.m. One who lives on the wages of iniquity, a venal or corrupt person (as one who takes bribes or other unlawful gains), a dishonest fellow, an idle, worthless fellow
s.f. The wages of iniquity, unlawful gains (e.g. peculation, malversation, &c.), dishonesty; the bread of idleness
v.t. To hold or keep sacred; to probibit, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Unlawfully begotten, illegitimate, spurious, base-born; — a bastard; a wicked person, a rogue, villain, scoundrel
s.f. Illegitimacy; — wickedness, roguery, rascality, villainy
(-se), To behave ill (to), ill-treat; to abuse
adj. (f. -ī), Unlawful; ill-gotten (as wealth, &c.); illegitimate, spurious; — ḥarām-kā pillā or nut̤fa, s.m. A bastard; a term of abuse
s.m. An evil-doer, a fornicator, an adulterer
s.f. Any forbidden act (but especially fornication and adultery)
v.t. To regard or determine as unlawful or wrong; — v.n. To do what is forbidden or wrong, to commit fornication or adultery
To receive the wages of iniquity
s.m. The spinal marrow; the spinal cord
s.f. A violent or unnatural death, suicide
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَرَام (harām), from Arabic حَرَام (ḥarām)

def: adj. Forbidden; sacred; — s.m. The sacred territory of Mecca; the temple of Mecca, or the court of the temple; a sanctuary; — s.f. A wife; a concubine, a female slave; any woman under covert; a daughter; — women's apartments, seraglio, harem
more: n. m. حرم was forbidden. 1. The sacred circuit round the temple of Mecca; a sanctuary. 2. (n. f.) A female slave who becomes a mistress; a kept-mistress; concubine. Ghar karo lauṅḍī ke dil meṅ, haj kī kyā hājit tumheṅ? Hai haram ghar meṅ, mīyāṅ, Kābe na jānā chāhīye! Nāznīn. Your dwelling in your slave's heart make, of pilgrimage no need, Your home's your sanctuary, my lord, to Mecca wherefore speed? haram-sarā, mahal-sarā , n. m. A harem; seraglio. haram yā lauṅḍī kā janā , Base-born; bastard ( harām-zādah ).
1. adj. (Heb. ) Sacred, forbidden. 2. s. m. The temple of Mecca, a sanctuary. 3. s. f. A concubine. 4. A daughter, womenʼs apartments, a seraglio, a harem.
subs: adj. & s.m. Base-born; — a bastard ( = ḥarām-zāda).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حرم (haram), from Arabic حَرَم (ḥaram).

def: bastard, illegitimate child; son of a bitch
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حرامزاده (harāmzāda).

def: s.m. dual (of ḥarām), The two sacred places (Mecca and Medina).
s.f. corr. of hiḍimbā, q.v. s.f. Name of the sister of Hiḍimb (she changed herself into a beautiful woman and married Bhīm, by whom she had a son named Ghaṭotkać); — name of the wife of Hanumat
subs: or hiḍimbā-ramaṇ, s.m. The lord, or the husband, of Hiḍimbā; names of Bhīm.

def: adj. & s.m. Unlawful, illegal; ill mate, spurious; — wicked, atrocious; — a doer of what is forbidden or unlawful, a wicked person; robber, an assassin; — a rascal, a cheat; — a bastard
more: n. m. A bastard. harāmī mūt , n. m. A bastard, a wicked person.
s. m. A robber, assassin, doer of what is forbidden or unlawful, rascal, cheat. حرامي پلا ḥarām-ī pillā, Bastard.
subs: or tikkā, or ṭol, or mūt, s.m. A bastard; a term of abuse.

def: s. f. Wickedness, villainy, rascality, illegitimacy.
s.f. contrac. of ḥarām-zādagī, q.v.s.v. ḥarām.

s.m. Taking, accepting; laying hold (of), seizing, seizure; taking away, removing; carrying off; rape; — stealing; theft; plunder; — subtraction; abstraction; — withholding; annulling; disregarding; — (in Arith.) dividing; division; — the hand; arm; — semen virile; — gold; — boiling water
more: s. m. A deer or antelope, hart.
s. m. 1. Theft, plunder, taking away by force. 2. Subtraction. 3. The hand. هرن دکهہ haraṇ-dukh, Taking away or dissipating grief or trouble.
subs: s.f. House of detention or correction; penitentiary
adj. Taking away pain or trouble, dissipating trouble or grief

def: part. (dialec.) = harā, perf. part. of harnā, q.v. suff. (f. -ī), added to infinitives to form, 1˚ part. fut. act., or 2˚ nouns of agency (the inf. aff., in Western Hindī, generally takes the form an or ne = Prk.अणअ or अणिअ = S. अनीय; in East. H. ani or ni = Prk. अणिअ, &c.), e.g. karan-hār, or karne-hār or hārā (E. H. karani-hār or hārā), 'about to do; one who is going to do; doer'; — hon-hār, or honi-hār, 'what is to be; possible; future.' In W. H. this suff. frequently takes the form wālā or wārā (euphonic w taking the place of h), e.g. dene-wālā or dene-wārā (f. -wālī, &c.), 'about to give; one who is about to give; a giver.' The suff. hār and hārā are frequently confounded with the secondary suff. wālā, q.v.; e.g. lakaṛ-hārā, 'a woodman.' (It would perhaps be more correct to regard these suff. as being ˚anhār, ˚anehār, ˚anihār, &c., and to consider them as attached to the roots of verbs; e.g. mar-anhār, fr. rt. mar; but the popular notion is that the suffixes are hār, &c., and are joined to infinitives; and hence, probably, the confusion between these suffixes and the suffix wālā.)
n. m. A paroquet.

ety: inf. n. of حرف 'to turn from, to alter,' &c.
def: s.m. Changing, altering; inverting, turning (as a coat); — extremity, verge, border, margin, brink, brow, side, edge; ridge or ledge (of a mountain); summit of a mountain; — nib (of a writing-reed) obliquely cut; a crooked pen; writing obliquely; — a letter of the alphabet; (in Gram.) an indeclinable word, a particle; — a word (so used in lexicons, &c.); — blame, censure, reproach, stigma, animadversion
more: n. m. pl. hurūf , 1. A letter of the alphabet. 2. ( Gram .) An indeclinable word; a particle. 3. Handwriting. harf uṭhānā , v. n. To read; decipher; make out. Ab to Aṅgrezī harf uṭhā letā hai . harf uṛnā , v. n. To be obliterated, washed out (ارَنا 7). harf-i-istisnā , A particle denoting exception. harf-āshnā , One who knows his letters or is just able to read. harf ānā , v. n. To be calumniated; to suffer a stain. harf biṭhānā, joṛnā, yā jamānā , v. a. To compose for printing. harf-ba-harf, harfan harfan , adv. Literatim ; letter for letter; syllable by syllable; word for word. harf banānā , v. a. 1. To alter or insert a letter. 2. To write well or carefully. 3. To alter a writing. 4. To tamper with (a document). harf pakaṛnā, harf-gīrī k. , v. a. 1. To find fault with; criticise. 2. To pick holes in; to cavil. harf pahchānnā , v. a. To learn the alphabet or one's letters. harf-i-tardī…

s. m. 1. A letter of the alphabet. 2. A particle (in grammar). 3. Inverting, changing, turning (as, a coat). 4. A crooked pen. 5. Writing diagonally. 6. A margin, side. 7. A word. 8. def: adj. = harā, q.v. suff. (f. -ī), added to infinitives to form, 1˚ part. fut. act., or 2˚ nouns of agency (the inf. aff., in Western Hindī, generally takes the form an or ne = Prk.अणअ or अणिअ = S. अनीय; in East. H. ani or ni = Prk. अणिअ, &c.), e.g. karan-hār, or karne-hār or hārā (E. H. karani-hār or hārā), 'about to do; one who is going to do; doer'; — hon-hār, or honi-hār, 'what is to be; possible; future.' In W. H. this suff. frequently takes the form wālā or wārā (euphonic w taking the place of h), e.g. dene-wālā or dene-wārā (f. -wālī, &c.), 'about to give; one who is about to give; a giver.' The suff. hār and hārā are frequently confounded with the secondary suff. wālā, q.v.; e.g. lakaṛ-hārā, 'a woodman.' (It would perhaps be more correct to regard these suff. as being ˚anhār, ˚anehār, ˚anihār, &c., and to consider them as attached to the roots of verbs; e.g. mar-anhār, fr. rt. mar; but the popular notion is that the suffixes are hār, &c., and are joined to infinitives; and hence, probably, the confusion between these suffixes and the suffix wālā.)
